reviews.review_text:
For me, Geometry Dash stopped being "just a rhythm game" a long time ago. Past a certain point, it turns into less of a game and more of a test of patience, consistency, and mental fortitude.

When I first bought it, I genuinely thought it was just a simple cube game with music playing in the background. After playing a few easy levels, I realized how wrong I was. The deeper you get into Geometry Dash, the better you understand why people sink thousands of hours into this game.

Right now, I’m at a level where I can consistently beat Hard Demons, and even this has completely shifted my perspective on the game. Levels no longer feel like random obstacles; it becomes a matter of muscle memory, timing, reflex control, and pure focus. You start recognizing patterns instantly, developing click consistency, understanding the sync better, and learning something from almost every single death. To an outsider, it might look like mindless repetition, but when you’re actually playing, every attempt feels like progress in some way.

The game's difficulty is brutal, but weirdly enough, it feels fair. Most of the time when you die, you know EXACTLY why you died. Maybe you panicked, clicked too early, lost focus for a split second, or choked because you got nervous near the end. And the strange thing is, that’s precisely what makes beating a difficult level so satisfying.

The only other game in my life that ever gave me this feeling was Elden Ring. That sensation of grinding for hours and finally overcoming something that seemed impossible... But to be honest, Geometry Dash makes me feel it even more intensely. Because here, there is absolutely nothing else to blame. No RNG, no teammates, no excuses — it’s just you, your reflexes, your consistency, and your own mistakes. Every new best you hit, every percentage you gain, and every completion you pull off feels truly earned.
One of the biggest reasons this game is still so alive after all these years is its community. The level editor is, honestly, insane. People create cinematic levels, boss fights, impossible challenges, memory demons, modern art-style levels, and effect levels that look like they shouldn't even be possible in this game.

The music gets way less credit than it deserves, too. After playing enough, certain songs become directly tied to specific levels, fails, progress memories, and that feeling of victory. Sometimes I’ll hear a song outside of the game, and the click patterns of a level will instantly pop into my head.

And honestly, one of the best parts about Geometry Dash is actually being able to see your own improvement. You don’t notice it at first, but one day you go back to a level that used to seem impossible, and suddenly, it feels easy. That feeling is extremely addictive.

Yes, the game is frustrating.
Yes, it can break your mentality.
Yes, when you say "one last attempt," it's never actually the last attempt.

But very few games can make self-improvement feel this real.

Geometry Dash takes one-button gameplay and turns it into one of the most satisfying, skill-based experiences I’ve ever played.

And do you want to know the worst part?

The exact moment you finish your hardest level, you immediately start looking for the next one.

What else can I say to one of the games that has shaped my overall life? This game earns a permanent spot in my heart even until I'm gone. Regardless of everything going on and how less I've been playing it recently, I still love this game <3

My dad gave me this game for my birthday.
I played for 4 hours straight and really liked it. 
I played the lite version previosly and it wasn't such a cool experience because i was on phone and there where ads, but i can understand that because it is a single developer game and he needs some cash of course. Now, i am playing the full version on pc, and that is WAY cooler, trust me. 

There is a problem tho... 

When creating an account, you need to have a gmail (google mail) adress like "someone@gmail.com", any other ones (school emails like "someone@school.com", work emails like "someone@workplace.com" and even outlook emails like "someone@outlook.com") won't work.
This game is very fun but frustrating at the same time. Also, use keyboard not mouse to click.
most goated classic historical mythical game ever

I know people will call me a trend hopper or maybe something else for getting it this year on steam but overall i love this game. It helps me pass time and it can be frustrating but the feeling of beating a level you've been hacking at is SO satisfying. I used to play lite on my phone years ago and i do not regret getting the real game.

Pretty fun little game. I've played it on mobile for years. It is a 2D side scroller platforming rhythm game that has some pretty decent music in it as well. It provides player created levels for the community to play, unlockable skins, it actually has different game modes, secret paths, a lot of achievements to go for and more.

Geometry Dash is one of those games that looks simple at first, but quickly becomes incredibly addictive. The gameplay is challenging, frustrating at times, but also extremely satisfying when you finally beat a difficult level.

The music fits perfectly with every level and makes the experience even more intense. One of the best parts of the game is the community content — there are thousands of custom levels, so there’s always something new to play.

You can really feel yourself improving over time, especially with timing and reflexes. Even after raging at impossible demons, I still keep coming back to the game. Definitely recommended for anyone who enjoys rhythm games, challenges, and amazing community creativity.
This game is absolute after 2.2! The levels are insane, and not only that but probably very fun with friends to see who can beat a level the fastest, 9/10 game!

My playtime doesn't say much but I play this on mobile (have over 1k hours there) and this game is incredibly fun. The robtop levels are good for starting out but the online levels is where its at. There's tens of thousands of levels made by many people, you can contribute and make a level yourself. You really should give it a try, at least try the Lite version, or World.

I've even shown my other younger cousins the game and they love it to. 10/10 Love ya RobTop keep doing what you do fr.

I love games that give you a challenge, especially when you can pace yourself and work up to greater achievements instead of getting trashed on as soon as you open the game. Another thing I adore about this game is how you are encouraged to create levels. Games where you can express your creativity with no limits are my favorite, which is what gives Geometry Dash a special place in my heart.

One of the best games out here... Here's why. One: The creativity, you get to create ANYTHING (if that's bad or not) as it allows players to make great platformer/levels, So far i created 300+ levels WITHOUT NOTICTING. that's how good the game is. Two: Simple, its simple and sweet, geometry dash is SOO simple... just a cube and cube jump over spikes, yet its soo good. allows for ANYTHING to be made, i played for 2 years on my phone and now i get to play PC. 2.8 hours now and im feeling great. Three: Since the game is so simple, it allows for POTATO PC'S to allow to play too! Sure lag but still playable! 10/10 and no Pay-To-Win. Truly a one time best purchase ever in my life. Recommended!
